title: Sentinel Shield bounded-command result
description: Machine-readable result of running one external process under a bounded wall-clock timeout via scripts/lib/bounded-process.sh (bp_run + bp_result_json). It classifies the outcome into a stable status and distinguishes a timed-out process (exit_code null, timed_out true) from a normal failure, an unavailable executable, and a signal death. It carries NO command arguments (which may contain credentials): 'command' is the executable BASENAME only, and the command's category is a stable coarse token.

command.description: The executable BASENAME only (e.g. 'docker', 'gh', 'grype'). NEVER the full command line — arguments may contain credentials and are never surfaced.

command_category.description: Stable coarse category driving the timeout default (e.g. 'docker-probe', 'github-api', 'scanner-version', 'scanner-exec', 'git-verify', 'package-install', 'package-probe', 'archive', 'consumer-validation').

status.description: success=command exited 0; failed=command exited non-zero; timed-out=exceeded the bounded timeout and was terminated (TERM then KILL); unavailable=the executable was not found on PATH (never launched); signalled=terminated by a signal not caused by our timeout.

exit_code.description: The preserved process exit code on success (0) or failed (non-zero). NULL on timed-out, unavailable, and signalled (no meaningful exit code).

signal.description: The terminating signal number when status=signalled; NULL otherwise.

timeout_seconds.description: The effective bounded timeout applied to this run, in seconds.

duration_seconds.description: Measured wall-clock duration of the run, in seconds.

timed_out.description: True iff the process was terminated by the bounded timeout. Redundant with status=timed-out; provided as a direct boolean for gates.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC time the result was produced.
